reasoning: Zohran Mamdani is the Democratic nominee in heavily Democratic New York City. The late October 2025 polling shows him with a massive 14-point lead (43.2%) in the general election over Independent Andrew Cuomo (28.9%) and Republican Curtis Sliwa (19.4%). Because the general election uses First-Past-The-Post voting (RCV is only for primaries), the opposition to Mamdani is split. For Mamdani to finish in 2nd place, he would have to lose the election, likely to Cuomo. Given the 14-point lead and the presence of Sliwa splitting the anti-Mamdani vote, the probability of Mamdani losing and falling to 2nd place is quite low. There is a small tail risk of extreme tactical voting by Sliwa supporters to stop the progressive Mamdani, but with early voting beginning soon and a solid base of support for the Democratic nominee, a Mamdani first-place finish is highly probable. I assess an 8% chance he is upset and lands in exactly second place.
batch_queries: Will Zohran Mamdani win second place in the 2025 NYC mayoral election?
